meta.note: Prayer/music program added 2026-07-07 from the FFL Teaching Guidelines Lingua Angelica Syllabus (pp. x-xi); 34 book lessons mapped unit-by-unit onto the app's 30 slots (tail items fold into unit reviews). Hymn full texts not included (live in Lingua Angelica).

lessons.title: Nouns; 1st Declension Singular
lessons.focus: Latin nouns; the 1st declension; nominative and accusative singular

prayer.title: Dona Nobis Pacem
prayer.latin: Dona nobis pacem.
prayer.english: Grant us peace.
prayer.note: This week: whole piece. (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 1; optional enrichment per guide.) Sung as a round; from the Agnus Dei.

lessons.title: 1st Declension Plural
lessons.focus: 1st declension plural endings; all five cases

prayer.title: Christus Vincit
prayer.latin: Christus vincit, Christus regnat, Christus imperat.
prayer.english: Christ conquers, Christ reigns, Christ rules.
prayer.note: This week: whole piece. (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 2; optional enrichment per guide.) Ancient acclamation; appropriate for Easter.

lessons.title: 1st Conjugation; Present Active Indicative
lessons.focus: The first conjugation; present tense active voice; principal parts

practical_latin.english: I love, you love, he loves

prayer.title: The Table Blessing
prayer.latin: Benedic, Domine, nos et haec Tua dona, quae de Tua largitate sumus sumpturi, per Christum Dominum nostrum. Amen.
prayer.english: Bless us, O Lord, and these Thy gifts, which from Thy bounty we are about to receive, through Christ our Lord. Amen.
prayer.note: This week: lines 1-2. (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 3; optional enrichment per guide.)

lessons.title: Sentences; Subject and Predicate
lessons.focus: Forming Latin sentences; subject (nominative) vs. direct object (accusative)

prayer.title: Gloria Patri
prayer.latin: Gloria Patri et Filio et Spiritui Sancto, sicut erat in principio et nunc et semper et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.
prayer.english: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.
prayer.note: This week: first 3 lines. (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 18; optional enrichment per guide.)

lessons.title: 1st-2nd Declension Adjectives
lessons.focus: Adjectives agree with nouns in gender, number, case; -us/-a/-um pattern

prayer.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
prayer.latin: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum cotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
prayer.english: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
prayer.note: This week: part 1 (LA p. 12). (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 21; optional enrichment per guide.)

lessons.title: Personal Pronouns; Is, Ea, Id
lessons.focus: Latin personal pronouns; is, ea, id as pronoun and adjective

prayer.note: This week: part 2 (LA p. 13). (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 22; optional enrichment per guide.)

lessons.title: Latin Sayings; Reading Passage
lessons.focus: Review of Unit IV; Bennett reading: Labores Herculis

prayer.title: Agnus Dei
prayer.latin: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, dona nobis pacem.
prayer.english: Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us. Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us. Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, grant us peace.
prayer.note: This week: whole prayer; review Pater Noster. (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 25-26; optional enrichment per guide.)

lessons.title: Review — Unit IV
lessons.focus: Review: adjectives, pronouns, perfect tense, 3rd declension adjectives

prayer.title: Kyrie (Greek)
prayer.latin: Kyrie eleison. Christe eleison. Kyrie eleison.
prayer.english: Lord, have mercy. Christ, have mercy. Lord, have mercy.
prayer.note: This week: whole prayer; review. (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 27-28; optional enrichment per guide.) Greek, not Latin — the one non-Latin text of the Mass ordinary.

lessons.title: Passive Voice — Present, Imperfect, Future
lessons.focus: The passive voice: the subject receives the action; passive endings

practical_latin.latin: Roma non uno die aedificata est.
practical_latin.english: Rome was not built in one day.

prayer.title: The Sanctus
prayer.latin: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus, Dominus Deus Sabaoth, pleni sunt caeli et terra gloria Tua. Hosanna in excelsis. Benedictus qui venit in nomine Domini. Hosanna in excelsis.
prayer.english: Holy, Holy, Holy, Lord God of Hosts, heaven and earth are full of Thy glory. Hosanna in the highest. Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord. Hosanna in the highest.
prayer.note: This week: first 3 lines. (Lingua Angelica syllabus, book lesson 29; optional enrichment per guide.) Includes the Benedictus ('Benedictus qui venit…').

lessons.title: 4th Declension

adeste-fideles.title: Adeste Fideles
adeste-fideles.note: Christmas hymn, learned verse by verse; full text and music in Lingua Angelica (not included here).

gloria-patri.title: Gloria Patri
gloria-patri.latin: Gloria Patri et Filio et Spiritui Sancto, sicut erat in principio et nunc et semper et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.
gloria-patri.english: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.

pater-noster.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
pater-noster.latin: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum cotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
pater-noster.english: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.

agnus-dei.title: Agnus Dei
agnus-dei.latin: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, dona nobis pacem.
agnus-dei.english: Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us. Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us. Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, grant us peace.

kyrie.title: Kyrie (Greek)
kyrie.latin: Kyrie eleison. Christe eleison. Kyrie eleison.
kyrie.english: Lord, have mercy. Christ, have mercy. Lord, have mercy.
kyrie.note: Greek, not Latin — the one non-Latin text of the Mass ordinary.

sanctus.title: The Sanctus
sanctus.latin: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus, Dominus Deus Sabaoth, pleni sunt caeli et terra gloria Tua. Hosanna in excelsis. Benedictus qui venit in nomine Domini. Hosanna in excelsis.
sanctus.english: Holy, Holy, Holy, Lord God of Hosts, heaven and earth are full of Thy glory. Hosanna in the highest. Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord. Hosanna in the highest.
